2606.25954.title: Measurable Majorities Are Not Finitely Axiomatizable
2606.25954.paper_summary: This theoretical note studies the finite axiomatizability of strict majority reasoning in finite social decision frames. Moss and Pedersen (2026) <doi: 10.48550/arXiv.2606.23853> introduce a coherence criterion that characterizes exactly when qualitative majority judgments are representable by a finitely additive measure. The question addressed here is whether that coherence criterion can be replaced, in the finite setting, by any bounded finite fragment. We prove that it cannot. For every $k\ge 1$, we construct a maximal standard frame whose shortest coherence violation has length exactly $2k+2$. Hence there is no uniform finite bound on the incoherence index of social decision frames, resolving Conjecture 5.7 stated by Moss and Pedersen (2026). The construction is geometric, in the sense that it proceeds via orthogonality and dimension in rational vector spaces, and self-contained: it isolates a symmetric family of half-sized voting blocs and extends it to a maximal frame in which every shorter balanced obstruction is excluded. Along the explicit infinite sequence of universe sizes obtained in the construction, this also establishes the middle-layer family predicted by Conjecture B.25 by Moss and Pedersen (2026). Together with the soundness and completeness theorem for the Moss-Pedersen minimal logic for strict majorities, this establishes that measurable social decision frames are not finitely axiomatizable in that language.
2606.25954.paper_summary_zh: 這篇理論筆記研究了在有限社會決策框架中嚴格多數推理的有限公理化。Moss 和 Pedersen (2026) <doi: 10.48550/arXiv.2606.23853> 提出了恰好描述何時質性多數判斷可以由有限可加測度表示的一致性標準。這裡探討的問題是，在有限情境中，是否可以用任何有界的有限片段來替代該一致性標準。我們證明這是不可能的。對於每個 $k\ge 1$，我們構造了一個最大標準框架，其最短的一致性違反的長度恰好為 $2k+2$。因此，社會決策框架的不一致指數沒有統一的有限界限，解決了 Moss 和 Pedersen (2026) 提出的猜想 5.7。這一構造是幾何性的，因為它通過有理向量空間中的正交性和維度進行，並且是自給自足的：它孤立了一個對稱的半大小投票區塊家族，並將其擴展到一個最大框架中，其中排除了每個更短的平衡障礙。在構造中獲得的顯式無窮序列的宇宙大小中，這也建立了 Moss 和 Pedersen (2026) 猜想 B.25 預測的中層家族。結合 Moss-Pedersen 嚴格多數的最小邏輯的健全性和完備性定理，這確立了可測社會決策框架在該語言中不是有限公理化的。
2606.25954.author: Lawrence S. Moss et.al.
2606.25954.authors: Lawrence S. Moss, Arthur Paul Pedersen
